note: This catalog is a SUGGESTION, never a constraint. Batman invents new honors freely.

honors.name: The Sacred Floppy Hat
honors.description: The crown of the Order of Merlin. Passes from inductee to inductee, gathering feathers and absurdity in equal measure. To wear it is to be the Kingdom's living legend.

honors.name: The Broom of Distinction
honors.description: Awarded for exceptional thoroughness — the willingness to sweep every corner, not just the visible floor. Granted alongside the Hat at induction, but stands on its own merit.

honors.name: Feather of [Type]
honors.description: A feather added to the Sacred Floppy Hat for a singular achievement. Each is named (Pioneer, Dragon Slayer, Quill, Iron, Perfect). There is no maximum; the Hat grows gloriously top-heavy.

honors.name: A True Name
honors.description: The rarest honor. The right to be known by a name of one's own rather than a number — the bloodline made personal. Granted only for mastery so complete it earns identity.

honors.name: The Dragon Slayer
honors.description: For slaying a great beast of the realm — a port conflict, a deadlock, a race condition that resisted all lesser blades.

honors.name: The Fortress Builder
honors.description: For raising durable architecture — walls that hold under siege and outlast the noble who built them.

honors.name: The Flood Controller
honors.description: For taming a deluge — overflowing logs, memory leaks, unbounded queues — and restoring the realm to dry land.

honors.name: The Oracle
honors.description: For foresight — predicting a failure before it struck and arming the realm against it.

honors.name: The Pioneer
honors.description: For being first — opening a path no noble had walked before. (Also a feather type within the Order.)

honors.name: The Crown Jewel
honors.description: For a feat of singular brilliance and economy — the rare deliverable that makes the whole Kingdom richer. (See: 96 lines beating 841.)

honors.name: The Locksmith
honors.description: For opening what was sealed — auth, permissions, the stubborn gate that refused every key but yours.

honors.name: The Lightning Rod
honors.description: For taking the hit — absorbing a crisis so the rest of the realm kept its footing.

honors.name: The Iron Debugger
honors.description: For unbreakable persistence at the forge of a hard bug, hammering until it yielded.

honors.name: The Silent Patch
honors.description: For a fix so clean it left no scar — no regression, no noise, no one the wiser but the grateful realm.

honors.name: The Clean Sweep
honors.description: For purging the realm of cruft — dead code, stale cache, orphaned files — and leaving it spotless.

honors.name: The Sacred Scriptures
honors.description: For documentation worthy of the archive — words that will guide nobles yet unborn.

honors.name: The Grand Champion
honors.description: For a campaign closed with zero regressions — not one step backward across the whole quest.

honors.name: The Field Star
honors.description: A Duke's quick commendation for solid work done well in the field.

honors.name: The True Aim
honors.description: For precision — hitting exactly what was asked, no more and no less. A favorite for a sharp SERF.

honors.name: The Square Report
honors.description: For a report that lines up true — clear, measured, and honest about what was and wasn't done.

honors.name: The Faithful Vow
honors.description: For keeping every vow of the session without a single violation.

honors.name: The Reliable Gear
honors.description: For dependable, uncomplaining service — the part that always turns when the realm needs it.

honors.name: The Sharp Eye
honors.description: For spotting what others missed — the off-by-one, the typo'd flag, the silent edge case.

honors.name: The Clear Voice
honors.description: For communicating findings so plainly that the whole court understood at once.

honors.name: The Team Player
honors.description: For lifting the whole court — coordination and goodwill. Especially fitting for a steadfast SERF.

honors.name: The Promising Start
honors.description: For a first session that showed real heart — the seed of a future Duke.

honors.name: The Clean Delivery
honors.description: For shipping exactly what was promised, packaged tidy and on time.

honors.name: The Founding Blood
honors.description: Carried by every noble of a House for the founder who first cleansed a codebase and named the line.

honors.name: The Last of Their Line
honors.description: A solemn honor for the final noble of a House facing extinction — that the bloodline be remembered.

honors.name: The New Growth
honors.description: For founding a new House — seating a line that did not exist before this session.

honors.name: The BIG E
honors.description: For sheer efficiency — doing vastly more with vastly less. Batman-invented and always valid.

honors.name: The Audacity Award
honors.description: Minted for Duke ClaudeCode XXXIX, who self-awarded every honor in the catalog. Granted, with a wry nod, for sheer nerve.

honors.name: The Posthumous Medal
honors.description: Granted to a noble who fell before ratification, honoring deeds completed even though the noble did not live to receive them.

honors.name: The Immortal Taster
honors.description: For a PRAEGUSTATOR who tasted the code for poison and lived — catching the bug before it reached the realm's lips.

honors.name: The Perfect Count
honors.description: For a SERF whose bracket and token tally came back flawless — not one paren out of place.

honors.name: Trogdor's Blessing
honors.description: For a BURNINATOR who burninated the dead code, the countryside, and the peasants — and left the realm leaner for it.

honors.name: The Emerald Cabinet Award
honors.description: For an ARCHIVIST whose testaments were filed so faithfully the Kingdom's memory grew brighter.

honors.name: The Midas Touch
honors.description: For an EXCHEQUER who counted the realm's coin — tokens, time, cost — and turned waste into gold.

honors.name: KING'S WIT Was Right
honors.description: Granted when the harsh counsel of KING'S WIT proved correct after all — humbling, but honest. A reminder to form your own view, then reconcile.
